Test Case Name: TC01_Fleet NB - Per Day Limit Field Not Mandatory - AB-SEF20U - English
Description: Verify that Per Day Limit field is not mandatory for 20 series forms (Fleet) during New Business transaction
Action:
1. Create submission in UWD for Commercial Auto - Fleet
2. Initiate and purge to Phoenix
3. Navigate to Line selection screen
4. Verify product details and confirm 20 series form (AB-SEF20U) is selected
5. Navigate to the form fields screen showing Per Day Limit field
6. Leave Per Day Limit field blank (do not enter any value)
7. Proceed through remaining mandatory fields
8. Price the submission
9. Quote the submission
Expected Result:
1. User should be able to create a Commercial Auto - Fleet submission
2. Line selection screen should display the 20 series form option
3. Per Day Limit field should be visible but NOT marked as mandatory (no red asterisk)
4. User should be able to proceed without entering a value in Per Day Limit field
5. Pricing and Quote should complete successfully
6. No validation error should appear for blank Per Day Limit field

2. Per Day Limit field should NOT be marked as mandatory in Policy Change

4. Pricing and Quote should complete successfully
5. No validation error should appear

Test Case Name: TC09_UI Validation - Per Day Limit Field Not Required - Visual Indicator Absence
Description: Verify that Per Day Limit field does not display as mandatory (no red asterisk or required indicator)
Action:
1. Create a new Commercial Auto submission (Fleet or IRCA)
2. Navigate to any 20 series form screen (e.g., AB-SEF20U, FANB20G, NSEF20U)
3. Locate the Per Day Limit field
4. Inspect the field for mandatory indicators
Expected Result:
1. Per Day Limit field should be visible on the form
2. Field should NOT have a red asterisk (*) indicating mandatory status
3. Field should NOT have any text saying 'Required' or similar
4. Field should be clearly marked as optional if any indicator is present

Test Case Name: TC10_UI Validation - Submission Allowed Without Per Day Limit Entry
Description: Verify that user can proceed through submission process without entering Per Day Limit value
Action:
1. Create a Commercial Auto submission (Fleet)
2. Navigate to form screen with Per Day Limit field
3. Do NOT enter any value in Per Day Limit field
4. Attempt to proceed to next screen or submit form
5. Verify no blocking validation errors appear
Expected Result:
1. User should be able to proceed without entering Per Day Limit
2. No mandatory field validation error should appear for Per Day Limit field
3. Form should accept the submission with blank Per Day Limit
4. No error message like 'Per Day Limit is required' should appear

Test Case Name: TC11_Document Validation - Printed Forms Show Blank Per Day Limit - Fleet NB
Description: Verify that when Per Day Limit is left blank, the printed form also displays blank for that field
Action:
1. Create and Issue a Commercial Auto - Fleet NB policy with AB-SEF20U form
2. Leave Per Day Limit field blank during creation
3. Complete all submission steps (Price, Quote, Bind, Issue)
4. Navigate to Forms screen in Issued policy
5. Download and view the printed form (AB-SEF20U)
6. Inspect the Per Day Limit field in the printed document
Expected Result:
1. Issued policy should be created successfully
2. Forms screen should display all printed forms
3. Downloaded form should display blank/empty in the Per Day Limit field
4. No default or placeholder values should appear in Per Day Limit on printed form

Test Case Name: TC12_Document Validation - Printed Forms Show Blank Per Day Limit - IRCA PC
Description: Verify that when Per Day Limit is left blank in Policy Change, the printed form also displays blank
Action:
1. Create and Issue a base Commercial Auto - IRCA policy

Test Case Name: TC13_Negative Test - All 20 Series Forms Support Non-Mandatory Per Day Limit
Description: Verify non-mandatory Per Day Limit functionality across all 8 required forms
Action:
1. Create Commercial Auto submissions for each form:
   - AB-SEF20U, FANB20G, NBEF20U, NL SEF20U, NSEF20U, OPCF20U, PE SEF20U, SEF20U
2. For each form, navigate to form fields
3. Leave Per Day Limit blank
4. Attempt to proceed/submit
Expected Result:
1. All 8 forms should allow blank Per Day Limit
2. No form should enforce Per Day Limit as mandatory
3. All submissions should proceed successfully without validation errors
4. Consistent behavior across all 20 series forms

Test Case Name: TC14_Negative Test - Per Day Limit Non-Mandatory in Multiple Languages
Description: Verify that Per Day Limit remains non-mandatory across English and French language selections
Action:
1. Create Commercial Auto submissions in both English and French
2. For each language, navigate to form screens with 20 series forms
3. Leave Per Day Limit blank in both languages
4. Process through submission steps
Expected Result:
1. English forms should allow blank Per Day Limit
2. French forms should also allow blank Per Day Limit (e.g., FANB 20 G "Privation De Jouissance")
3. No language-specific mandatory validation should apply
4. Both language versions should treat Per Day Limit as non-mandatory
